title: Melt in your mouth Ukrainian pork Skillet
description: A hearty and flavorful Ukrainian dish featuring tender pork, roasted to perfection in a cast-iron skillet, with a blend of aromatic spices and khus khus, sure to please any crowd. This recipe is a crowd-pleaser and can be served at any time of the day.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the score fat in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes to the skillet and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the khus khus, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Cover the skillet with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork in the oven for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the pork hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
